They prioritize functionality (e.g., flexibility in shoes, machine-washable materials) and environmental concerns (preferring natural cotton over synthetics) over high-fashion or technical aesthetics (Conv 55, 59).

In summary, the user tends to avoid attention-grabbing or overly "stylish" items, favoring basic, practical, and versatile pieces that allow them to blend into various environments comfortably.
